signals.name: New Infrastructure Project Announced

evidence.finding: Bagdad (AZ): new concentrating facilities announced. $3.5B capital investment, 200-250M lbs/yr additional copper. Investment decision targeted H2 2026.
El Abra (Chile): major mill facility announced. 700M+ lbs/yr copper capacity potential.

signals.notes: Two confirmed project announcements. Bagdad is US-based (primary target). El Abra is international (secondary). Both require substantial HDPE slurry and process water pipeline infrastructure.

signals.name: Construction Permit Filed

evidence.finding: El Abra (Chile): Environmental Impact Statement submitted to Chilean regulators March 2026. EIS = pre-permit stage in Chile. US Bagdad permit not yet filed — investment decision still pending H2 2026.

signals.notes: EIS filed for El Abra = regulatory process underway, not confirmed construction permit. Bagdad US permit will follow H2 2026 investment decision. Classified as Inferred — real but not yet confirmed permit. Upgrade to C when Bagdad FID announced.

signals.name: Federal or State Infrastructure Funding Awarded
signals.notes: No federal grant or IIJA funding found for FCX US mines. Mining operations are typically privately funded. Not applicable for this account type — private capex model, not grant-dependent.

signals.name: RFQ / Tender Issued for Piping Materials
signals.notes: Track B limitation — no access to SAM.gov or private RFQ portals during research. FCX procurement is likely handled through direct supplier relationships for mine-site materials. Recommend manual check of DemandStar and direct FCX procurement portal.

signals.name: New Engineering or Procurement Leader Hired (last 18 months)
signals.notes: No leadership change found in earnings releases or press. CEO Kathleen Quirk and Chairman Richard Adkerson both long-tenure. LinkedIn not accessible during Track B. Manual LinkedIn check recommended for VP Capital Projects, VP Engineering, Director of Procurement at FCX Bagdad or corporate level.

signals.name: Facility Expansion or Mine Scale-Up Announced

evidence.finding: Bagdad (AZ): Expansion from current capacity + 200-250M lbs/yr copper. New concentrating facilities required. $3.5B capex.
Grasberg Block Cave (Indonesia): Infrastructure ramp-up underway. 65% capacity H2 2026, 80% by mid-2027, full capacity end-2027.

signals.notes: Multiple confirmed expansions. Bagdad is the primary US opportunity for ISCO. Each new concentrating facility at a copper mine requires extensive HDPE slurry pipeline, process water recycling systems, and tailings management piping.

signals.name: M&A Activity

evidence.finding: 8-K filed January 14, 2026 — Asset acquisition or business combination. Full content not extracted.

signals.notes: 8-K signals a material transaction but content not confirmed. Classified as Inferred until the filing is reviewed. Upgrade to C if acquisition involves physical mine assets in the US.

signals.name: Data Center Development Announced
signals.notes: Not applicable. FCX is a mining company, not a data center operator.

signals.name: EPA or State Water Quality Mandate / Compliance Deadline

evidence.finding: Mining operations in AZ and NM are subject to EPA water quality regulations (Clean Water Act, Mine Water Treatment requirements). Tailings management = active regulatory compliance area. FCX tailings reports published annually.

signals.notes: Inferred — mining companies face ongoing water treatment and pipeline compliance requirements. No specific enforcement action or deadline found. Classified as I, not C, without a specific EPA order. Tailings HDPE liner systems are a standard compliance requirement — provides angle for ISCO.

signals.name: Capital Budget Approved or Increased

evidence.finding: 2026 CapEx: $4.3 billion total. $3.0B for major projects (including $1.4B underground development + $1.6B discretionary growth). Q1 2026 actual: $1.0B already spent.

signals.notes: Strongest possible financial signal. $4.3B capex confirmed in earnings report filed with SEC. Bagdad alone = $3.5B if approved. Capital committed and already being deployed ($1.0B in Q1 alone).

signals.name: PE Ownership or PE-Backed

evidence.finding: FCX is publicly traded (NYSE: FCX). No PE ownership.

signals.notes: Public company — PE signal not applicable. Not a negative for the account.

signals.name: Carbon Reduction / Sustainability Commitment Published

evidence.finding: 2025 Annual Report on Sustainability published. CDP questionnaire participation confirmed. Climate action, water stewardship, and biodiversity programs active.

signals.notes: Public company with full ESG reporting. HDPE pipe has significantly lower lifecycle carbon footprint vs. steel alternatives — direct angle for ISCO's sustainability pitch.

signals.name: Incumbent HDPE Supplier Known
signals.notes: No incumbent supplier identified. Track B limitation. ISCO has supplied comparable copper mining projects (Collahuasi, Chile) — can reference this in outreach as proof of sector expertise.

signals.name: Competitor HDPE Supplier Recently Won Project
signals.notes: Unknown. No competitor wins found in public sources. Needs manual investigation.

distraction_signals.name: CEO Accepting Outside Role
distraction_signals.evidence: CEO Kathleen Quirk — no outside roles found. Chairman Richard Adkerson — no outside roles found.

distraction_signals.name: Financial Distress or Bankruptcy
distraction_signals.evidence: $4.3B capex 2026, quarterly dividends declared, NYSE-listed. No distress indicators.

distraction_signals.name: Project Cancelled or Capex Freeze
distraction_signals.evidence: No cancellations found. Bagdad decision H2 2026 = project live, not cancelled.
